description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a light blue fuselage and wings, featuring prominent roundels on the upper and lower wings, reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The airplane has two main wings stacked vertically, supported by struts, and a single wooden propeller mounted at the nose. The tail section includes a horizontal stabilizer and a vertical fin, both adorned with red, white, and blue striping. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a brown section indicating the pilot's seating area. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable, suitable for grass or dirt runways.
description: A vintage light blue biplane with stacked wings, roundels, an open cockpit, and a wooden propeller. The tail features red, white, and blue stripes, and the landing gear is fixed.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stacked biplane wings, roundels, and the open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full wingspan, roundels on both wings, and the tail's striped pattern.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with clear visibility of the propeller, wing struts, and cockpit placement.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the wooden propeller, engine cowling, and the symmetrical biplane wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; features classic rudder and elevator surfaces.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
